root_text.an3.126:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno yena kapilavatthu tadavasari.
root_text.an3.126:1.2: Assosi kho mahānāmo sakko: “bhagavā kira kapilavatthuṁ anuppatto”ti.
root_text.an3.126:1.3: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:2.1: “Gaccha, mahānāma, kapilavatthusmiṁ, tathārūpaṁ āvasathaṁ jāna yatthajja mayaṁ ekarattiṁ vihareyyāmā”ti.
root_text.an3.126:2.2: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavato paṭissutvā kapilavatthuṁ pavisitvā kevalakappaṁ kapilavatthuṁ anvāhiṇḍanto nāddasa kapilavatthusmiṁ tathārūpaṁ āvasathaṁ yatthajja bhagavā ekarattiṁ vihareyya.
root_text.an3.126:3.1: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:3.2: “natthi, bhante, kapilavatthusmiṁ tathārūpo āvasatho yatthajja bhagavā ekarattiṁ vihareyya.
root_text.an3.126:3.3: Ayaṁ, bhante, bharaṇḍu kālāmo bhagavato purāṇasabrahmacārī.
root_text.an3.126:3.6: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavato paṭissutvā yena bharaṇḍussa kālāmassa assamo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā santharaṁ paññāpetvā udakaṁ ṭhapetvā pādānaṁ dhovanāya yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:3.7: “santhato, bhante, santhāro, udakaṁ ṭhapitaṁ pādānaṁ dhovanāya.
root_text.an3.126:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā yena bharaṇḍussa kālāmassa assamo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an3.126:4.6: Sve dānāhaṁ bhagavantaṁ payirupāsissāmī”ti bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.an3.126:5.1: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko tassā rattiyā accayena yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:5.2: “tayo khome, mahānāma, satthāro santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.126:5.4: Idha, mahānāma, ekacco satthā kāmānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti;
root_text.an3.126:5.5: na rūpānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti, na vedanānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti.
root_text.an3.126:5.6: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco satthā kāmānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti, rūpānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti;
root_text.an3.126:5.8: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco satthā kāmānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti, rūpānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti, vedanānaṁ pariññaṁ paññāpeti.
root_text.an3.126:5.9: Ime kho, mahānāma, tayo satthāro santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.126:5.10: Imesaṁ, mahānāma, tiṇṇaṁ satthārānaṁ ekā niṭṭhā udāhu puthu niṭṭhā”ti?
root_text.an3.126:6.1: Evaṁ vutte, bharaṇḍu kālāmo mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:6.3: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:6.5: Dutiyampi kho bharaṇḍu kālāmo mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:6.7: Dutiyampi kho bhagavā mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:6.9: Tatiyampi kho bharaṇḍu kālāmo mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:6.11: Tatiyampi kho bhagavā mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.126:7.2: “mahesakkhassa vatamhi mahānāmassa sakkassa sammukhā samaṇena gotamena yāvatatiyaṁ apasādito.
root_text.an3.126:7.4: Atha kho bharaṇḍu kālāmo kapilavatthumhā pakkāmi.
root_text.an3.126:7.5: Yaṁ kapilavatthumhā pakkāmi tathā pakkantova ahosi na puna paccāgacchīti.

translation_text.an3.126:1.1: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans when he arrived at Kapilavatthu.
translation_text.an3.126:1.2: Mahānāma the Sakyan heard that he had arrived.
translation_text.an3.126:1.3: He went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.an3.126:2.1: “Go into Kapilavatthu, Mahānāma, and check if there’s a suitable guest house where I can spend the night.”
translation_text.an3.126:2.2: “Yes, sir,” replied Mahānāma. He returned to Kapilavatthu and searched all over the city, but he couldn’t see a suitable guest house for the Buddha to spend the night.
translation_text.an3.126:3.1: Then Mahānāma went up to the Buddha, and said to him,
translation_text.an3.126:3.2: “Sir, there’s no suitable guest house in Kapilavatthu for you to spend the night.
translation_text.an3.126:3.3: But there is this Bharaṇḍu the Kālāma, who used to be the Buddha’s spiritual companion.
translation_text.an3.126:3.4: Why don’t you spend the night at his hermitage?”
translation_text.an3.126:3.5: “Go, Mahānāma, and set out a mat.”
translation_text.an3.126:3.6: “Yes, sir,” replied Mahānāma. He went to Bharaṇḍu’s hermitage, where he set out a mat, and got foot-washing water ready. Then he went back to the Buddha and said to him,
translation_text.an3.126:3.7: “The mat and foot-washing water are set out.
translation_text.an3.126:3.8: Please, sir, go at your convenience.”
translation_text.an3.126:4.1: Then the Buddha went to Bharaṇḍu’s hermitage, sat down on the seat spread out,
translation_text.an3.126:4.4: “It’s too late to pay homage to the Buddha today.
translation_text.an3.126:4.6: Tomorrow I’ll pay homage to the Buddha.” He bowed to the Buddha and respectfully circled him, keeping him on his right, then he left.
translation_text.an3.126:5.1: Then, when the night had passed, Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an3.126:5.2: “Mahānāma, there are these three teachers found in the world.
translation_text.an3.126:5.4: One teacher advocates the complete understanding of sensual pleasures,
translation_text.an3.126:5.5: but not of forms or feelings.
translation_text.an3.126:5.6: One teacher advocates the complete understanding of sensual pleasures and forms,
translation_text.an3.126:5.8: One teacher advocates the complete understanding of sensual pleasures, forms, and feelings.
translation_text.an3.126:5.9: These are the three teachers found in the world.
translation_text.an3.126:5.10: Do these three teachers have the same goal or different goals?”
translation_text.an3.126:6.1: When he said this, Bharaṇḍu said to Mahānāma,
translation_text.an3.126:6.5: For a second time, Bharaṇḍu said,
translation_text.an3.126:6.9: For a third time, Bharaṇḍu said,
translation_text.an3.126:7.2: “The Buddha has rebuked me three times in front of this illustrious Mahānāma.
translation_text.an3.126:7.4: Then Bharaṇḍu the Kālāma left Kapilavatthu,
